speaker: WARWICK
text: I wonder how the King escaped our hands.

speaker: YORK
text: While we pursued the horsemen of the north,

speaker: YORK
text: He slyly stole away and left his men;

speaker: YORK
text: Whereat the great lord of Northumberland,

speaker: YORK
text: Whose warlike ears could never brook retreat,

speaker: YORK
text: Cheered up the drooping army; and himself,

speaker: YORK
text: Lord Clifford, and Lord Stafford, all abreast,

speaker: YORK
text: Charged our main battle’s front and, breaking in,

speaker: YORK
text: Were by the swords of common soldiers slain.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Lord Stafford’s father, Duke of Buckingham,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Is either slain or wounded dangerous.

speaker: EDWARD
text: I cleft his beaver with a downright blow.

speaker: EDWARD
text: That this is true, father, behold his blood.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: And, brother, here’s the Earl of Wiltshire’s blood,

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: Whom I encountered as the battles joined.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Speak thou for me, and tell them what I did.

speaker: YORK
text: Richard hath best deserved of all my sons.

speaker: YORK
text: But is your Grace dead, my lord of Somerset?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Such hope have all the line of John of Gaunt!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Thus do I hope to shake King Henry’s head.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And so do I, victorious prince of York.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Before I see thee seated in that throne

speaker: WARWICK
text: Which now the house of Lancaster usurps,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I vow by heaven these eyes shall never close.

speaker: WARWICK
text: This is the palace of the fearful king,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And this the regal seat. Possess it, York,

speaker: WARWICK
text: For this is thine and not King Henry’s heirs’.

speaker: YORK
text: Assist me, then, sweet Warwick, and I will,

speaker: YORK
text: For hither we have broken in by force.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: We’ll all assist you. He that flies shall die.

speaker: YORK
text: Thanks, gentle Norfolk. Stay by me, my lords.—

speaker: YORK
text: And soldiers, stay and lodge by me this night.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And when the King comes, offer him no violence

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unless he seek to thrust you out perforce.

speaker: YORK
text: The Queen this day here holds her parliament,

speaker: YORK
text: But little thinks we shall be of her council.

speaker: YORK
text: By words or blows, here let us win our right.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Armed as we are, let’s stay within this house.

speaker: WARWICK
text: “The Bloody Parliament” shall this be called

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unless Plantagenet, Duke of York, be king

speaker: WARWICK
text: And bashful Henry deposed, whose cowardice

speaker: WARWICK
text: Hath made us bywords to our enemies.

speaker: YORK
text: Then leave me not, my lords; be resolute.

speaker: YORK
text: I mean to take possession of my right.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Neither the King nor he that loves him best,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The proudest he that holds up Lancaster,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Dares stir a wing if Warwick shake his bells.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I’ll plant Plantagenet, root him up who dares.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Resolve thee, Richard; claim the English crown.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My lords, look where the sturdy rebel sits,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Even in the chair of state! Belike he means,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Backed by the power of Warwick, that false peer,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To aspire unto the crown and reign as king.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Earl of Northumberland, he slew thy father,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And thine, Lord Clifford, and you both have vowed revenge

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: On him, his sons, his favorites, and his friends.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: If I be not, heavens be revenged on me!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: The hope thereof makes Clifford mourn in steel.

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: What, shall we suffer this? Let’s pluck him down.

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: My heart for anger burns. I cannot brook it.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be patient, gentle Earl of Westmorland.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Patience is for poltroons such as he.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: He durst not sit there had your father lived.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: My gracious lord, here in the Parliament

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Let us assail the family of York.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Well hast thou spoken, cousin. Be it so.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, know you not the city favors them,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And they have troops of soldiers at their beck?

speaker: EXETER
text: But when the Duke is slain, they’ll quickly fly.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Far be the thought of this from Henry’s heart,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To make a shambles of the Parliament House!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Cousin of Exeter, frowns, words, and threats

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall be the war that Henry means to use.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou factious Duke of York, descend my throne

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And kneel for grace and mercy at my feet.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I am thy sovereign.

speaker: YORK
text: I am thine.

speaker: EXETER
text: For shame, come down. He made thee Duke of York.

speaker: YORK
text: It was my inheritance, as the earldom was.

speaker: EXETER
text: Thy father was a traitor to the crown.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Exeter, thou art a traitor to the crown

speaker: WARWICK
text: In following this usurping Henry.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Whom should he follow but his natural king?

speaker: WARWICK
text: True, Clifford, that’s Richard, Duke of York.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And shall I stand, and thou sit in my throne?

speaker: YORK
text: It must and shall be so. Content thyself.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Be Duke of Lancaster. Let him be king.

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: He is both king and Duke of Lancaster,

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: And that the lord of Westmorland shall maintain.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And Warwick shall disprove it. You forget

speaker: WARWICK
text: That we are those which chased you from the field

speaker: WARWICK
text: And slew your fathers and, with colors spread,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Marched through the city to the palace gates.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Yes, Warwick, I remember it to my grief;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And by his soul, thou and thy house shall rue it.

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: Plantagenet, of thee and these thy sons,

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: Thy kinsmen, and thy friends, I’ll have more lives

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: Than drops of blood were in my father’s veins.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Urge it no more, lest that, instead of words,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: I send thee, Warwick, such a messenger

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: As shall revenge his death before I stir.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Poor Clifford, how I scorn his worthless threats!

speaker: YORK
text: Will you we show our title to the crown?

speaker: YORK
text: If not, our swords shall plead it in the field.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What title hast thou, traitor, to the crown?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy father was as thou art, Duke of York;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy grandfather, Roger Mortimer, Earl of March.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I am the son of Henry the Fifth,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Who made the Dauphin and the French to stoop

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And seized upon their towns and provinces.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Talk not of France, sith thou hast lost it all.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The Lord Protector lost it and not I.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When I was crowned, I was but nine months old.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You are old enough now, and yet, methinks, you lose.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Father, tear the crown from the usurper’s head.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Sweet father, do so. Set it on your head.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: Good brother, as thou lov’st and honorest arms,

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: Let’s fight it out and not stand caviling thus.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sound drums and trumpets, and the King will fly.

speaker: YORK
text: Sons, peace!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Peace thou, and give King Henry leave to speak!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Plantagenet shall speak first. Hear him, lords,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And be you silent and attentive too,

speaker: WARWICK
text: For he that interrupts him shall not live.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Think’st thou that I will leave my kingly throne,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Wherein my grandsire and my father sat?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No. First shall war unpeople this my realm;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, and their colors, often borne in France,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And now in England to our heart’s great sorrow,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Shall be my winding-sheet. Why faint you, lords?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My title’s good, and better far than his.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Prove it, Henry, and thou shalt be king.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Henry the Fourth by conquest got the crown.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Twas by rebellion against his king.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I know not what to say; my title’s weak.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Tell me, may not a king adopt an heir?

speaker: YORK
text: What then?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: An if he may, then am I lawful king;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For Richard, in the view of many lords,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Resigned the crown to Henry the Fourth,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose heir my father was, and I am his.

speaker: YORK
text: He rose against him, being his sovereign,

speaker: YORK
text: And made him to resign his crown perforce.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Suppose, my lords, he did it unconstrained,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Think you ’twere prejudicial to his crown?

speaker: EXETER
text: No, for he could not so resign his crown

speaker: EXETER
text: But that the next heir should succeed and reign.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Art thou against us, Duke of Exeter?

speaker: EXETER
text: His is the right, and therefore pardon me.

speaker: YORK
text: Why whisper you, my lords, and answer not?

speaker: EXETER
text: My conscience tells me he is lawful king.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: All will revolt from me and turn to him.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Plantagenet, for all the claim thou lay’st,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Think not that Henry shall be so deposed.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Deposed he shall be, in despite of all.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Thou art deceived. ’Tis not thy southern power

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Of Essex, Norfolk, Suffolk, nor of Kent,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Which makes thee thus presumptuous and proud,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Can set the Duke up in despite of me.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: King Henry, be thy title right or wrong,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Lord Clifford vows to fight in thy defense.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: May that ground gape and swallow me alive

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Where I shall kneel to him that slew my father.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O Clifford, how thy words revive my heart!

speaker: YORK
text: Henry of Lancaster, resign thy crown.—

speaker: YORK
text: What mutter you, or what conspire you, lords?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Do right unto this princely Duke of York,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Or I will fill the house with armèd men,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And over the chair of state, where now he sits,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Write up his title with usurping blood.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My lord of Warwick, hear but one word:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let me for this my lifetime reign as king.

speaker: YORK
text: Confirm the crown to me and to mine heirs,

speaker: YORK
text: And thou shalt reign in quiet while thou liv’st.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I am content. Richard Plantagenet,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Enjoy the kingdom after my decease.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: What wrong is this unto the Prince your son!

speaker: WARWICK
text: What good is this to England and himself!

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: Base, fearful, and despairing Henry!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: How hast thou injured both thyself and us!

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: I cannot stay to hear these articles.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Nor I.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Come, cousin, let us tell the Queen these news.

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: Farewell, faint-hearted and degenerate king,

speaker: WESTMORLAND
text: In whose cold blood no spark of honor bides.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Be thou a prey unto the house of York,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And die in bands for this unmanly deed.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: In dreadful war mayst thou be overcome,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Or live in peace abandoned and despised!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Turn this way, Henry, and regard them not.

speaker: EXETER
text: They seek revenge and therefore will not yield.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, Exeter!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why should you sigh, my lord?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Not for myself, Lord Warwick, but my son,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whom I unnaturally shall disinherit.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text:
But be it as it may.
                 
                (
                To
                 
                York
                .
                )
               I here entail

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The crown to thee and to thine heirs forever,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Conditionally, that here thou take an oath

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To cease this civil war and, whilst I live,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To honor me as thy king and sovereign,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And neither by treason nor hostility

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To seek to put me down and reign thyself.

speaker: YORK
text: This oath I willingly take and will perform.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Long live King Henry! Plantagenet, embrace him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And long live thou and these thy forward sons!

speaker: YORK
text: Now York and Lancaster are reconciled.

speaker: EXETER
text: Accursed be he that seeks to make them foes.

speaker: YORK
text: Farewell, my gracious lord. I’ll to my castle.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And I’ll keep London with my soldiers.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And I to Norfolk with my followers.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: And I unto the sea, from whence I came.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And I with grief and sorrow to the court.

speaker: EXETER
text: Here comes the Queen, whose looks bewray her anger.

speaker: EXETER
text: I’ll steal away.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Exeter, so will I.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Nay, go not from me. I will follow thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Be patient, gentle queen, and I will stay.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Who can be patient in such extremes?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ah, wretched man, would I had died a maid

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And never seen thee, never borne thee son,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Seeing thou hast proved so unnatural a father.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Hath he deserved to lose his birthright thus?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Hadst thou but loved him half so well as I,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or felt that pain which I did for him once,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or nourished him as I did with my blood,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou wouldst have left thy dearest heart-blood there,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Rather than have made that savage duke thine heir

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And disinherited thine only son.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Father, you cannot disinherit me.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: If you be king, why should not I succeed?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Pardon me, Margaret.—Pardon me, sweet son.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The Earl of Warwick and the Duke enforced me.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Enforced thee? Art thou king and wilt be forced?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I shame to hear thee speak. Ah, timorous wretch,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou hast undone thyself, thy son, and me,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And giv’n unto the house of York such head

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As thou shalt reign but by their sufferance!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To entail him and his heirs unto the crown,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What is it but to make thy sepulcher

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And creep into it far before thy time?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Warwick is Chancellor and the lord of Callice;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Stern Falconbridge commands the Narrow Seas;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The Duke is made Protector of the realm;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And yet shalt thou be safe? Such safety finds

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The trembling lamb environèd with wolves.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Had I been there, which am a silly woman,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The soldiers should have tossed me on their pikes

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Before I would have granted to that act.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But thou preferr’st thy life before thine honor.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And seeing thou dost, I here divorce myself

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Both from thy table, Henry, and thy bed,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Until that act of Parliament be repealed

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Whereby my son is disinherited.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The northern lords that have forsworn thy colors

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Will follow mine if once they see them spread;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And spread they shall be, to thy foul disgrace

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And utter ruin of the house of York.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thus do I leave thee.—Come, son, let’s away.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Our army is ready. Come, we’ll after them.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Stay, gentle Margaret, and hear me speak.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou hast spoke too much already. Get thee gone.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Gentle son Edward, thou wilt stay with me?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ay, to be murdered by his enemies!

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: When I return with victory from the field,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: I’ll see your Grace. Till then, I’ll follow her.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Come, son, away. We may not linger thus.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Poor queen! How love to me and to her son

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Hath made her break out into terms of rage!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Revenged may she be on that hateful duke,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose haughty spirit, wingèd with desire,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Will cost my crown, and like an empty eagle

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Tire on the flesh of me and of my son.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The loss of those three lords torments my heart.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I’ll write unto them and entreat them fair.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, cousin, you shall be the messenger.

speaker: EXETER
text: And I, I hope, shall reconcile them all.
